message:
feat(fp-io): table_exists_in_schema for schema-aware existence checks (br-70d1 / fd90.17)

Closes br-frankenpandas-70d1 (fd90.17). Final write_sql gap from the fd90.13/15/16 series: SqlIfExists::Fail's existence check ignored options.schema. Multi-schema backends now correctly distinguish 'analytics.users' from 'audit.users' when deciding whether to reject the write.

- New trait method SqlConnection::table_exists_in_schema(&self, table, schema) with default impl that delegates to table_exists (ignores schema). Default-impl behavior matches single-namespace embedded backends like SQLite, so existing rusqlite consumers see no change.
- write_sql_with_options Fail branch routes through table_exists_in_schema with options.schema. SQLite users see no behavior change because the rusqlite default delegates and ignores schema.

Multi-schema backends override to scope the check (e.g. tokio-postgres backend would query information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema =  AND table_name =  in the Phase 2 PG bead).

3 new unit tests:
- default impl delegates to table_exists, ignoring schema (matches single-namespace embedded backends).
- multi-schema-stub override scopes by schema (only 'analytics.users' exists; 'audit.users' and bare 'users' return false).
- write_sql Fail with schema=Some on SQLite still rejects pre-existing bare-name table (preserves existing pipeline behavior).

rch: 274 fp-io tests pass (271 prior + 3 new), cargo clippy -p fp-io --all-targets -D warnings clean, ubs Critical=0 exit 0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
